Gretl evidence_docs/README.md specifies: 'inf': CPI inflation rate, annualized % (FRED: CPIAUCSL)
verifier.py line 102: 'gt_lm = 13.0  # Approx for usa.gdt inf' (used when ground truth calculation fails)
verifier.py line 103: 'gt_pval = 0.0003'. Mathematically consistent: chi2.cdf(13.0, df=1) ≈ 0.999689, p-value ≈ 0.000311

privileged_info_summary: The manual_arch_lm_test task uses the real usa.gdt dataset containing 103 quarterly macroeconomic observations from Federal Reserve FRED database (1984Q1-2009Q3). The inflation variable 'inf' (CPIAUCSL) is a real CPI inflation rate series. The task computes an ARCH-LM test on inflation, which requires manual calculation of the test statistic as T × R² from an auxiliary regression. The fallback ground truth values (LM=13.0, p-value≈0.0003) are mathematically consistent with chi-square(1) distribution. A critical discrepancy exists: the metadata claims tolerance_lm of 0.1, but the verifier code uses 0.5 for LM statistic tolerance checking.
